testContractImpactSweep.purpose: Require proactive test-contract review whenever repository work changes, replaces, migrates, refactors, relocates, regenerates or reshapes implementation contracts.

scope.appliesTo: Any repository change that may alter a test-observed or user-observed contract.
scope.examples: Implementation changes, migrations, refactors, generated-output changes, route changes, component changes, data-shape changes, workflow changes, build-pipeline changes, asset-pipeline changes and documentation examples that tests or users depend on.

mandatoryRules.rule: Before claiming a pull request is ready for review, testing, merge or release, perform a proactive test-contract impact sweep for the change.
Treat tests, fixtures, snapshots, generated artefacts and shared assertion files as part of the contract surface, not as downstream failures to discover one at a time.
Search for legacy contract terms introduced or affected by the change before relying on CI logs.
Update all directly affected tests and assertions in the same change set as the implementation when the expected contract has intentionally changed.
Inspect generated output as well as source when templates, macros, compilers, formatters, code generators or build steps may change the committed artefact shape.
Prefer structural assertions over brittle exact strings when formatting, generation or framework output may legitimately alter whitespace, attribute order, wrapping or line breaks.
If a legacy assertion is intentionally retained, document the compatibility contract it protects.

searchChecklist.item: Old route ids, route names, path names and query parameters.
Old data attributes, ARIA attributes, DOM ids and DOM hooks.
Old generated file paths, generated artefact manifests and build target paths.
Old script URLs, stylesheet URLs, import paths and module preload paths.
Old API endpoints, request payload keys, response shape keys and event names.
Old telemetry keys, storage keys, binding names, feature flags and configuration keys.
Old fixture names, snapshot text, selector strings and accessibility assertions.
Old workflow names, workflow branch filters, job names, status-check names and release-gate assumptions.
Old documentation examples, comments and operational runbook references that tests or users may rely on.

readyForReviewGate.rule: A PR-ready summary must state whether a test-contract impact sweep was performed.
If the sweep found no affected tests, the summary must say how that was determined.
If affected tests remain unchanged, the summary must explain why the existing assertions still represent the intended contract.
